title: 7 Tracing Interac e-TransferГӮВ® Transactions
body_text:
7	Tracing Interac e-Transfer
ГӮВ®
Transactions
7.1	Overview of Tracing Interac
e-Transfer Transactions
Before submitting an Interac e-Transfer trace request, consult the EMCU, EMCD, and ECCX reports to verify the status of the Interac e-Transfer transaction. If your financial institution uses the Interac e-Transfer
Online Administration System (OAS), use the OAS search feature to verify the real-time status of transactions or customer transaction limits. See the Interac e-Transfer
Online Administration System (OAS) Part for more information.
If the status of the Interac e-Transfer transaction is blocked, escalate to your corporate security team. If youГўВҖВҷre part of your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs corporate security team, refer to the Fraud Alert System (FAS). For more information about fraud security, see Fraud Alert System, Online Banking User Guide, Form 9285, and Fraud Solutions ГўВҖВ“ Interac
ГӮВ®
e-Transfer Fraud Types and Processes, Form 9305. These documents are only available to authorized users.
If the status of the Interac e-Transfer transaction is requested, refer to the e-TransferГўВҖВҷs Interac Response Code for additional information. See Chapter 9, Interac e-Transfer Response Codes, for more information.
7.2	Tracing Interac
e-Transfer Transactions in ServiceNow
If youГўВҖВҷre unable to verify the status of the Interac e-Transfer by using the Interac e-Transfer
Online Administration System (OAS) or by consulting the EMCU, EMCD, and ECCX reports, submit a trace request in ServiceNow.
Proceed as follows to submit an Interac e-Transfer trace request:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 1.
Note: 	YouГўВҖВҷll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if itГўВҖВҷs your first login to the Client Centre, youГўВҖВҷve deleted your cookies, or youГўВҖВҷre using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 1: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications
. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Select
ServiceNow
. See Figure 3.
Note: 	Applications are grouped by type (e.g., Admin & Support, Payments). If needed, scroll down the page to find the application. If youГўВҖВҷve saved the application as a favourite, it will also appear under ГўВҖВңMy FavouritesГўВҖВқ.
Figure 3: Accessing ServiceNow
Select
Service Catalogue
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Select Service Catalogue
Select
Electronic Payments
, and then select
Interac
e-Transfer Trace Request
. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Select Interac e-Transfer Trace Request
Complete the Interac
e-Transfer Trace Request form as shown in FigureВ 6. You can trace a single transaction or multiple transactions.
ГўВ‘
Trace Request
ГўВҖВ“ Enter details to help clarify the purpose of the trace request.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Branch Name
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the name of your branch.
ГўВ‘Вў
Transit Number
ГўВҖВ“ Enter your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs transit number.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Reference Number
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the Interac Ref ID No. displayed on the ECCX report.
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Email Address
ГўВҖВ“ Enter your customerГўВҖВҷs email address.
ГўВ‘ВҘ
Transaction Date
ГўВҖВ“ Select the date of the transaction.
ГўВ‘ВҰ
Transaction Amount
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the amount of the transaction.
ГўВ‘В§
Select the Plus sign (
+
) to add another row for additional entries.
ГўВ‘ВЁ
Select the ГўВҖВңFlag as Potential FraudГўВҖВқ box if applicable.
ГўВ‘В©
Add attachments if applicable.
ГўВ‘ВӘ
Select
Submit
. The request is sent to Central 1 and a ServiceNow ticket number is created.
Figure 6: Interac e-Transfer Trace Request in ServiceNow
If ServiceNow isnГўВҖВҷt available, contact Central 1:

, Option 1
